name: Doris's Pralines
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all ingredients except butter, vanilla, and pecans.
2. Cook on low heat until the mixture forms a soft ball when dropped into cold water.
3. Remove from heat and add butter, vanilla, and pecans. Beat until the mixture holds its shape.
4. Spoon onto prepared wax paper (place old newspaper under the wax paper first).
5. If the candy becomes too hard before all is spooned out, add a little water and continue heating gently.
6. Alternatively, let it stand for a moment while spooning out.
7. Enjoy freshly made pralines made from Louisiana pecans – they truly make life worthwhile.
Note: Doris is the much better half (wife) of my good friend Gordon Martin, the sheriff of St. James Parish. Every Christmas Eve we visit them after watching the bonfires burned along the levees of the Mississippi River to light the way for Papa Noel (Santa Claus).
